name: Participant Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant enrollment → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through diverse participation → Strategic: Improved generalizability of findings
options: Diversify recruitment channels to include online platforms and social media
Implement targeted outreach programs for high-risk populations
Establish a referral network with sleep clinics and neurologists across Germany
review: Controls participant burden vs. data quality. The options fail to consider the potential impact on participant mental health.

name: Data Sharing Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Increased public awareness of sleep disorders → Systemic: Enhanced collaboration among researchers and clinicians → Strategic: Improved treatment outcomes through data-driven insights
options: Implement a tiered access system for de-identified physiological data
Establish partnerships with pharmaceutical companies to share data and develop new treatments
Create an open-access database for researchers and clinicians
review: Controls data privacy vs. public benefit. The options fail to consider the potential consequences of sharing sensitive participant information.

name: Staffing Model
consequences: Immediate: Improved staff satisfaction and retention → Systemic: Enhanced research quality through specialized staff → Strategic: Increased efficiency through optimized staffing ratios
options: Implement a rotating shift schedule to ensure adequate coverage
Hire additional staff members with expertise in sleep neurophysiology and computational neuroscience
Establish a mentorship program for junior researchers
review: Controls staffing costs vs. research quality. The options fail to consider the potential impact on staff burnout and turnover.

name: Technology Integration
consequences: Immediate: Improved data collection efficiency → Systemic: Enhanced research quality through advanced analytics → Strategic: Increased scalability through cloud-based infrastructure
options: Implement a cloud-based data storage system for enhanced accessibility
Develop an in-house software solution for event-triage tool development
Establish partnerships with technology companies to integrate emerging technologies
review: Controls technological costs vs. research quality. The options fail to consider the potential risks of relying on external vendors.

name: Funding Model
consequences: Immediate: Improved funding stability → Systemic: Enhanced research quality through dedicated resources → Strategic: Increased sustainability through diversified revenue streams
options: Diversify funding sources to include government grants and private donations
Establish a crowdfunding campaign for participant compensation and staffing costs
Implement a tiered access system for de-identified physiological data
review: Controls funding stability vs. research quality. The options fail to consider the potential risks of relying on single-source funding.

name: Participant Recruitment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant enrollment rates → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through more diverse participant profiles → Strategic: Improved Aim 2 phenotyping analysis accuracy.
options: Conservative: Leverage existing clinical networks and recruitment channels
Moderate: Implement targeted online advertising and social media campaigns
Radical: Develop a novel, AI-powered participant identification system using machine learning algorithms on electronic health records.
review: Controls Participant Enrollment Rates vs. Ensuring Diverse Participant Profiles. The options fail to consider the potential impact of participant demographics on data quality and Aim 2 phenotyping analysis accuracy.

name: Data Management and Sharing Protocol
consequences: Immediate: Improved data storage and retention → Systemic: Enhanced data sharing capabilities through standardized formats → Strategic: Increased collaboration opportunities with other research groups.
options: Conservative: Utilize existing EEG-BIDS-compatible format for data storage and sharing
Moderate: Implement a cloud-based data management system with secure access controls
Radical: Develop an open-source, blockchain-based data management platform for decentralized data sharing.
review: Controls Data Sharing vs. Ensuring Data Security. The options fail to consider the potential risks of data breaches and unauthorized access in the context of sensitive participant information.

name: Event-Triage Tool Development
consequences: Immediate: Reduced manual review burden → Systemic: Improved event-triage tool accuracy through machine learning → Strategic: Enhanced collaboration opportunities with other research groups.
options: Conservative: Develop and refine existing semi-automated event-triage tools
Moderate: Implement a hybrid approach combining human scoring and machine learning algorithms
Radical: Design an autonomous, AI-powered event-triage system using deep learning techniques.
review: Controls Event-Triage Accuracy vs. Ensuring Human Oversight. The options fail to consider the potential risks of relying solely on machine learning for event triage and the importance of human oversight in ensuring accuracy.

name: Participant Compensation and Incentives
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant engagement → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through more motivated participants → Strategic: Improved Aim 2 phenotyping analysis accuracy.
options: Conservative: Maintain existing per-night compensation of €80 plus meals
Moderate: Implement a tiered compensation system based on participant participation and engagement
Radical: Develop a novel, AI-powered incentive system using personalized rewards and feedback.
review: Controls Participant Motivation vs. Ensuring Fair Compensation. The options fail to consider the potential impact of participant motivation on data quality and Aim 2 phenotyping analysis accuracy.

name: Governance and Oversight Structure
consequences: Immediate: Improved governance and oversight → Systemic: Enhanced accountability through regular progress reviews → Strategic: Increased credibility and trustworthiness.
options: Conservative: Maintain existing governance structure with a 3-member external scientific advisory board
Moderate: Implement a hybrid approach combining internal and external oversight
Radical: Design an autonomous, AI-powered governance system using blockchain technology.
review: Controls Governance vs. Ensuring Accountability. The options fail to consider the potential risks of relying solely on internal governance and the importance of external oversight in ensuring accountability.

name: Material Adaptation Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Increased participant recruitment rates → Systemic: Enhanced data quality through standardized protocols → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Enhanced participant compensation
Streamlined pre-admission pathway
Implementing a matched control group
review: Controls participant satisfaction vs. study efficiency. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of participant burden on data quality.

name: Operational Scalability Plan
consequences: Immediate: Increased operational capacity through optimized staffing ratios → Systemic: Enhanced data annotation throughput and reduced false alarm rates → Strategic: Improved research outcomes and publication potential.
options: Implementing a tiered monitoring system
Enhanced PSG program with technician-flagged events
Development of semi-automated event-triage tools
review: Controls staffing ratios vs. participant safety. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact of staff turnover on data quality.
